programs.name: State Supplementary Payment (SSP)
programs.what_it_does: SSP provides additional monthly cash payments to eligible Supplemental Security Income (SSI) recipients to help cover essential living expenses like food and housing. It supplements the federal SSI benefit, making California's total payment higher than the federal amount alone.[1][4]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 65 or older, blind, or disabled individuals receiving SSI.[1][4]
programs.whats_unique: California adds its own state-funded supplement to federal SSI, boosting the monthly benefit (e.g., maximum combined ~$943 in 2020); not all states provide such a supplement at this level.[1][4]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by living situation; up to several hundred dollars monthly beyond federal SSI (e.g., combined max $943.72 in 2020).[4]

programs.name: Property Tax Postponement Program
programs.what_it_does: This program allows eligible homeowners to defer property tax payments, with the state covering the taxes as an interest-free loan repaid upon sale or transfer of the property. It reduces immediate financial burden for seniors on fixed incomes.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Homeowners aged 62 or older with low income and limited assets.[1]
programs.whats_unique: California's unique deferral option postpones taxes until property sale/transfer without interest, unlike standard federal programs or typical state exemptions.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Full postponement of property taxes (value depends on home assessment); repaid later with property sale proceeds.

programs.name: In-Home Supportive Services (IHSS)
programs.what_it_does: IHSS provides in-home personal care and supportive services like bathing, dressing, housekeeping, and meal preparation to help seniors remain safely in their homes. Medi-Cal funds these services as an alternative to nursing home care.[3][5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors and disabled adults eligible for Medi-Cal who need assistance with daily activities.[3][5]
programs.whats_unique: California's large-scale, Medi-Cal funded program emphasizes home-based care over institutionalization, serving far more recipients than typical state programs.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Up to 283 hours/month of paid in-home care (average ~$137,000/year equivalent for long-term needs, but in-home).[5]

programs.name: Caregiver Resource Centers (CRC)
programs.what_it_does: CRCs offer comprehensive support to family caregivers, including counseling, respite care, support groups, training, and care planning for conditions like Alzheimer's. Services are provided at low or no cost statewide.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of seniors with Alzheimer's, stroke, Parkinson's, or similar conditions.[2]
programs.whats_unique: California is the first state with a comprehensive, statewide network of CRCs ensuring every resident has local access; no equivalent national network exists.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Free or low-cost services including respite care (hours vary), counseling, and training.

programs.name: Multipurpose Senior Services Program (MSSP)
programs.what_it_does: MSSP provides case management, counseling, and community-based services to help at-risk seniors avoid nursing home placement. It coordinates care for independent living at home.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Medi-Cal eligible seniors aged 65+ at risk of nursing facility placement.[4]
programs.whats_unique: California-specific Medi-Cal waiver program focused on comprehensive assessment and home-based alternatives to institutional care.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Case management and services valued at preventing costly nursing home stays (average $137,000/year).[5]
